This image appears to show an ornamental tarantula from the Poecilotheria genus, often called a "tiger spider" due to its distinct stripes.
These spiders are native to India and Sri Lanka and are known for their fast movements and arboreal, or tree-dwelling, lifestyle.
Many species in this genus, such as Poecilotheria regalis or Poecilotheria rajaei, are recognized for their large size, with leg spans sometimes reaching up to 8 inches.
All species within the Poecilotheria genus are protected under CITES (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora).
